entries.title: High-End Hood Is More Autonomous Than Earlier Nomu

context.label: High-End Nomu Hood attacks during Endeavor and Hawks meeting

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hood shows greater autonomy, speech, tactical aggression, and combat complexity than many earlier Nomu.
content.injection: Treat Hood as a serious evolved Nomu threat. It can pressure Endeavor and Hawks through adaptation, mobility, and aggression.

entries.title: High-End Fight Is Public Symbol Test

context.label: High-End Nomu Hood attacks during Endeavor and Hawks meeting

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: The Hood fight tests Endeavor’s ability to act as public Number One under direct observation.
content.injection: Stage the High-End fight as symbolic crisis as well as combat. People are watching whether Endeavor can stand where All Might once did.

entries.title: Hawks Prioritizes Rescue And Support During Hood Fight

context.label: Endeavor and Hawks fight Hood in public view

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks uses Fierce Wings to rescue civilians, gather information, support Endeavor, and create openings rather than simply trade blows.
content.injection: In the Hood fight, Hawks should be fast support and rescue logistics. He multiplies Endeavor’s chances without replacing Endeavor’s role.

entries.title: Endeavor Must Fight Through Injury And Heat Limits

context.label: Endeavor and Hawks fight Hood in public view

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor’s Hellflame power is immense, but his body, overheating, and injuries matter during the Hood fight.
content.injection: Do not make Endeavor physically limitless. His victory should feel costly, hot, painful, and driven by refusal to fall.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia
sourceInfo.notes: Draft Saga Lorecard; requires human canon review before reference release.

ability.name: Hellflame
constraints: Endeavor can overheat and suffer serious injury.
High-output techniques require positioning and cost.
antiLore: Do not treat Hellflame as consequence-free unlimited firepower.

context.label: Endeavor and Hawks fight Hood in public view

content.fact: Hood’s regeneration and body manipulation force Endeavor to escalate toward a decisive burn rather than routine capture.
content.injection: Make Hood difficult to finish. Its regeneration and body structure should demand destructive, high-risk response.

entries.title: Hawks Feather Supply Can Be Depleted

context.label: Endeavor and Hawks fight Hood in public view

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks’s feathers are powerful but finite in an extended fight, especially when many are used for evacuation and support.
content.injection: Track Hawks’s feather economy. His mobility and rescue capacity should degrade if he spends too many feathers.

entries.title: Endeavor Victory Is Not Clean Redemption

context.label: Endeavor defeats Hood and raises his fist

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor’s public victory over Hood is a meaningful start, but it does not erase his family abuse or instantly make him beloved.
content.injection: After Hood, society may rally around Endeavor, but his family and moral debt remain unresolved.

sourceInfo.title: My Hero Academia

entries.title: Raised Fist Echoes All Might But Feels Different

context.label: Endeavor defeats Hood and raises his fist

content.fact: Endeavor raising his fist after defeating Hood echoes All Might’s symbol, but the emotional meaning is rougher and unresolved.
content.injection: Use the raised fist as a public symbol attempt, not a perfect replacement for All Might’s smile.

content.fact: Civilians see Endeavor pushed, wounded, and still fighting, which changes the emotional register of his Number One role.
content.injection: Let bystanders respond to both fear and recognition. Endeavor’s vulnerability matters because he keeps standing publicly.

content.fact: Dabi appears after the Hood fight, but his identity as Toya Todoroki is not revealed in this Context.

entries.title: Hawks Uses Casual Speech To Control Tension

context.label: Hawks draws Endeavor into investigating Nomu rumors

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Hawks often uses casual speech, irreverence, and humor to control tension while reading people closely.
content.injection: Write Hawks as verbally light but mentally sharp. His jokes must not erase his surveillance instincts.

entries.title: Endeavor Speech Is Blunt And Self-Pressuring

context.label: Endeavor and Hawks fight Hood in public view

continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: Endeavor tends toward blunt, self-pressuring speech rather than warmth or symbolic comfort.
content.injection: Give Endeavor direct, severe, effort-driven dialogue. He can be sincere without sounding like All Might.

entries.title: Hood Fight Should Not Include Later Paranormal Liberation Front Knowledge

context.label: High-End Nomu Hood attacks during Endeavor and Hawks meeting

continuity.sourceBoundary: My Hero Academia manga chapters 1-430. Chapter 431 'More' is treated as an optional post-series extra overlay unless explicitly loaded.
continuity.notes: Anime-only scenes, movies, OVAs, Vigilantes, Team-Up Missions, light novels, games, and filler material are excluded from the primary deck family unless added as separate overlay decks.

content.fact: During the Pro Hero arc, heroes do not yet understand the later Paranormal Liberation Front structure or Shigaraki’s coming transformation.
content.injection: Keep Hood fight knowledge limited to Nomu escalation and suspicious villain movement. Do not import PLF organization or war preparations yet.
